name: StackBlitz
description: Online IDE for instant web development environments in the browser
longDescription:
StackBlitz is an online IDE focused on web development. It runs Node.js and the full npm ecosystem directly inside the browser using WebContainers, so projects boot in a few seconds without spinning up a remote VM.

It offers ready-made templates for frameworks like React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, Next.js, Nuxt, Astro, and Vite, plus support for plain HTML/CSS/JS. Projects come with a live preview, devserver, terminal, and shareable URLs, which makes it convenient for reproducible bug reports and tutorials.

StackBlitz is widely used by framework authors and library maintainers to ship interactive docs, by educators for hands-on lessons, and by developers for quick experiments. Bolt, its AI app builder, generates and edits StackBlitz projects from natural-language prompts.

features.title: WebContainers runtime
features.description: Node.js and npm run entirely in the browser tab, so projects start in seconds with no remote VM.

features.title: Framework templates
features.description: First-class starters cover React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, Next.js, Nuxt, Astro, Vite, and more.

features.title: Live preview and terminal
features.description: An integrated devserver, terminal, and console make it easy to run, debug, and tweak projects.

features.title: Shareable project URLs
features.description: Every project has a stable URL ideal for bug reproductions, code reviews, and tutorial embeds.

features.title: Bolt AI app builder
features.description: Bolt generates and edits StackBlitz projects from natural-language prompts and deploys them.

useCases: Prototyping a new web app idea without local setup
Creating reproducible bug reports for open-source libraries
Embedding live code samples in framework documentation
Teaching web development with hands-on exercises in the browser

faq.question: Is StackBlitz free?
faq.answer: StackBlitz has a free tier for public projects; paid plans add private projects, larger team features, and higher Bolt usage.

faq.question: Do I need an account?
faq.answer: You can open and try projects without an account, but saving, forking, and creating private projects require signing up.

faq.question: Which frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: Templates exist for React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, Next.js, Nuxt, Astro, Vite, and more, with full Node.js available for custom setups.

faq.question: Does StackBlitz work offline?
faq.answer: Some WebContainer projects can keep running once loaded, but creating new projects and saving changes generally requires an internet connection.
